title: Cottages and Semi-Bungalows, Design No. 208 (first and second floor plans)
creator: J. W. Lindstrom (architect/publisher)
rightsholder_or_source: Internet Archive (Public Domain Mark 1.0); originally J. W. Lindstrom, Minneapolis, 1920
research_notes: Floor-plan plate is page n8 of the scan. Plan No. 208; second-floor sewing room and two bedrooms; first-floor living room, dining room, kitchen, plus the rounded sun porch.
research_features: Curved-wall test case: a semicircular bowed SUN PORCH and a curved bay on the dining/sewing rooms. First and second floor plans, room dimensions labeled. Good for testing non-rectilinear room boundaries.
feature_highlights: semicircular bowed sun porch with a true curved outer wall
canted/angled bay projecting over the porch on the second floor
chamfered projecting bay on the dining/sewing rooms
first and second floor plans on one plate
central straight-run stair tying the two floors
first-floor bedroom in addition to upstairs bedrooms
bath with plumbing fixtures drawn in
blue-line blueprint rendering on cream stock
supported_examples: straight walls at right angles for the conventional rooms
rectangular rooms derived from walls with labels and sizes
raster underlay tracing of the blueprint
roadmap_examples: multi-floor management (stacked first and second floors)

gap_features.why: the sun porch outer wall is a true semicircular arc and the dining/upper bays are bowed/canted, none of which the straight-segment wall model can represent
the glazed sun porch is an open roofed space, not a fully wall-enclosed interior room
